curriculum.subject: English Language and Literature
curriculum.overallDescription: 3 units, 4 chapters, and 2 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage, Discursive passage of 400-450 words (10 marks), Case-based factual passage with visual input of 200-250 words (10 marks), Comprehension, Interpretation, Analysis, Inference, Evaluation, Vocabulary
topics: Case-based factual passage with visual input of 200-250 words (10 marks)

units.description: Grammar, Writing Skills, Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech (10 marks), Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) (5 marks), Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words) (5 marks), Accuracy, Spelling, Punctuation, Creative Expression, Reasoning, Fluency, Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words)
topics: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech (10 marks)

topics: Short Answer Type Questions from Beehive (12 marks)
Short Answer Type Questions from Moments (6 marks)

subtopics: Case-based factual passage with visual input of 200-250 words (10 marks)

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Assessment
practicals.details: Students undergo assessment of Listening and Speaking Skills (05 marks) involving regular practice, interaction, and performance in activities like role play and group work.

activities.title: Reading Comprehension Practice
activities.details: Students engage in reading discursive passages (400-450 words) and case-based factual passages with visual inputs to practice comprehension, interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, and vocabulary usage.

activities.title: Grammar Accuracy Drill
activities.details: Students practice gap-filling, editing, and transformation exercises focusing on Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, and Reported speech to ensure accuracy in spelling, punctuation, and grammar.

activities.title: Descriptive Writing Workshop
activities.details: Students write descriptive paragraphs (100-120 words) based on visual or verbal cues describing a person, event, or situation to develop creative expression and appropriate style.

activities.title: Creative Storytelling Session
activities.details: Students compose a story based on a given cue/title or write a diary entry (100-120 words) to practice creative expression and fluency.

activities.title: Literary Context Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry, answering MCQs to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, appreciation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Literature Discussion Circle
activities.details: Students participate in peer interactions to answer short and long answer questions from Beehive and Moments, defending views and expressing ideas on themes, plots, and characters.

activities.title: Role Play and Skit Performance
activities.details: Students engage in role plays, skits, or dramatizations based on texts from Beehive and Moments to enhance listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Literary Project
projects.details: Students create art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, or Dramatization derived from the prescribed texts (Beehive and Moments) to demonstrate understanding and creativity.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Section A: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage (600-700 words total)
1. Discursive passage (400-450 words) - 10 Marks
2. Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words) - 10 Marks
Section B: Grammar (10 Marks) and Writing Skills (10 Marks)
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech (Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation)
Writing: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) - 5 Marks
Writing: Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words) - 5 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature (40 Marks)
Reference to the Context: Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry (MCQ/Objective Type) - 10 Marks
Short Answer Questions: Beehive (4 out of 5, 40-50 words) - 12 Marks
Short Answer Questions: Moments (2 out of 3, 40-50 words) - 6 Marks
Long Answer Questions: Beehive (1 out of 2, 100-120 words) - 6 Marks
Long Answer Questions: Moments (1 out of 2, 100-120 words) - 6 Marks
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary
Writing Skills and Grammar: Creative expression, reasoning, justifying, illustrating, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central theme/sub-theme, understanding writer's message
internal_assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment: 5 Marks
Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking activities including Role Play and Group Work
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization

competency_groups.title: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage
competency_groups.description: Assessment of conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting and vocabulary skills through discursive and case-based factual passages.

competency_groups.title: Grammar and Writing Skills
competency_groups.description: Assessment of accurate use of spelling, punctuation, grammar, creative expression, reasoning, justifying, illustrating, appropriate style and tone, using appropriate format and fluency.

competency_groups.title: Reference to the Context
competency_groups.description: Assessment of interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, appreciation and vocabulary through extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry.

competency_groups.title: Short & Long Answer Questions
competency_groups.description: Assessment of interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, creativity, imagination and extrapolation beyond the text across the text through questions from Beehive and Moments.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Assessment
practicals.details: Students undergo assessment of Listening and Speaking Skills (05 marks) involving regular practice, interaction, and performance in activities like role play and group work.

activities.title: Reading Comprehension Practice
activities.details: Students engage in reading discursive passages (400-450 words) and case-based factual passages with visual inputs to practice comprehension, interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, and vocabulary usage.

activities.title: Grammar Accuracy Drill
activities.details: Students practice gap-filling, editing, and transformation exercises focusing on Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, and Reported speech to ensure accuracy in spelling, punctuation, and grammar.

activities.title: Descriptive Writing Workshop
activities.details: Students write descriptive paragraphs (100-120 words) based on visual or verbal cues describing a person, event, or situation to develop creative expression and appropriate style.

activities.title: Creative Storytelling Session
activities.details: Students compose a story based on a given cue/title or write a diary entry (100-120 words) to practice creative expression and fluency.

activities.title: Literary Context Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry, answering MCQs to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, appreciation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Literature Discussion Circle
activities.details: Students participate in peer interactions to answer short and long answer questions from Beehive and Moments, defending views and expressing ideas on themes, plots, and characters.

activities.title: Role Play and Skit Performance
activities.details: Students engage in role plays, skits, or dramatizations based on texts from Beehive and Moments to enhance listening and speaking skills.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Literary Project
projects.details: Students create art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, or Dramatization derived from the prescribed texts (Beehive and Moments) to demonstrate understanding and creativity.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Section A: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage (600-700 words total)
1. Discursive passage (400-450 words) - 10 Marks
2. Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words) - 10 Marks
Section B: Grammar (10 Marks) and Writing Skills (10 Marks)
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech (Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation)
Writing: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) - 5 Marks
Writing: Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words) - 5 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature (40 Marks)
Reference to the Context: Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry (MCQ/Objective Type) - 10 Marks
Short Answer Questions: Beehive (4 out of 5, 40-50 words) - 12 Marks
Short Answer Questions: Moments (2 out of 3, 40-50 words) - 6 Marks
Long Answer Questions: Beehive (1 out of 2, 100-120 words) - 6 Marks
Long Answer Questions: Moments (1 out of 2, 100-120 words) - 6 Marks
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary
Writing Skills and Grammar: Creative expression, reasoning, justifying, illustrating, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying central theme/sub-theme, understanding writer's message
internal_assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment: 5 Marks
Art-integrated projects (Role Play, Skit, Dramatization) must be used
practical_assessment: Listening and Speaking activities including Role Play and Group Work
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization
